parameters.description: Advanced search filter object that scopes which awards or subawards are listed.
Required list of API-defined field labels to return in each row. Valid labels vary by award-versus-subaward mode and award type family.
Maximum rows returned for the page.
Sort direction for the selected sort key.
1-indexed page number for regular pagination.
Requested sort field label. If omitted, the server sorts by the first requested field.
constraints: Invalid combinations of sort field, award_type_codes, and spending_level are rejected.

documented.notes: Valid options are any of the fields in the response row objects.

observed.defaultBehavior: Source code sets sort to fields[0] when omitted.

parameters.description: Legacy switch to group by subawards instead of awards.
Cursor component for the experimental Elasticsearch search_after pagination flow.
Sort-value cursor component paired with last_record_unique_id for experimental Elasticsearch search_after pagination.
Mode selector that chooses award rows or subaward rows and therefore which field catalog and data source are used.

observed.defaultBehavior: When omitted in a live probe, the response returned spending_level=awards.

templates.name: award-search-baseline
templates.description: List prime-award rows for a bounded contract search using a small field set; when present, derive the documented response-side semantic fact canonical_award_lookup_id from generated_internal_id rather than relying only on the display Award ID.

templates.name: high-value-award-screening
templates.description: Template for high_value_award_screening: bounded contract-only screen that sorts by Award Amount descending so the first rows are top-dollar candidates instead of alphabetical Award ID order. Even though the visible fields array stays small, the intended downstream handoff field is the response-side semantic fact canonical_award_lookup_id, derived from generated_internal_id on each returned prime-award row.

templates.name: subaward-search-baseline
templates.description: List subaward rows with prime-award linkage fields.

templates.name: award-search-after-page
templates.description: Continue sequential Elasticsearch pagination by replaying the same request with the page_metadata cursor pair from the prior page.

response.shapeSummary: Live award-mode probing confirmed an available paginated JSON response with spending_level, limit, results, page_metadata, and messages. Each award row contains the requested field-label columns plus helper identifiers such as internal_id, generated_internal_id, awarding_agency_id, and agency_slug; for prime-award rows, this bundle also exposes the response-side semantic fact canonical_award_lookup_id, derived from generated_internal_id for downstream award drilldowns.

fields.description: Echoed or defaulted spending_level for the response rows.
Page-size value echoed by the endpoint.

Array of award or subaward rows keyed by the requested field labels plus helper identifiers; for prime-award rows, this bundle also exposes canonical_award_lookup_id as a response-side semantic alias derived from generated_internal_id.
Pagination metadata for the current slice.
Warnings or directives returned with the current request.

observed.notes: Observed a search-floor warning about the earliest searchable date of 2007-10-01.

fields.description: Current page number.
Whether another page exists.
Search-after cursor component for the next sequential page.
Search-after sort-value cursor component for the next sequential page.
Internal numeric award id for prime-award rows or string subaward identifier for subaward rows.

Generated unique award id helper column for prime-award rows. This bundle uses the returned value to expose the response-side semantic fact results[].canonical_award_lookup_id and carries that same business key unchanged into downstream award-detail or award-funding requests even though those related endpoints label it differently.

observed.notes: Reviewer-backed drilldown evidence showed a row with display Award ID HT940216C0001 and generated_internal_id CONT_AWD_HT940216C0001_9700_-NONE-_-NONE-; that generated value carried successfully into both award detail and award funding follow-ups. This bundle exposes the same helper as the response-side semantic fact results[].canonical_award_lookup_id so agents can treat the downstream handoff key as part of the documented response contract.

fields.description: Response-side semantic fact for prime-award drilldowns. Derive this documented handoff value from generated_internal_id even when the visible fields array only requested display columns such as Award ID, Recipient Name, Award Amount, and Awarding Agency.

documented.notes: Not a literal API response key; this bundle normalizes the observed generated_internal_id helper into an explicit downstream handoff fact.

observed.notes: Reviewer-backed live screening showed this handoff value arriving as generated_internal_id even though the template requested only four visible fields.

constraints: Semantic alias, not a literal transport key; derive it from generated_internal_id when present.
Carry this value unchanged into v2__awards__award_id path award_id or v2__awards__funding body award_id.
Do not replace it with the display Award ID when the generated helper is available.

fields.description: Human-facing award identifier label such as a PIID, FAIN, or URI. Use it for display or filtering, but do not treat it as the canonical_award_lookup_id when generated_internal_id is present.
Recipient name for award-mode rows.

examples: STATE STREET BANK AND TRUST COMPANY

fields.description: Award amount for contracts and non-loan assistance rows.
Awarding agency display name.

examples: Department of Housing and Urban Development

fields.description: Agency profile id returned alongside Awarding Agency.
Agency slug returned alongside Awarding Agency when an agency profile page exists.
Total outlays for applicable award types.
Procurement award type label.
Assistance award type label.
Award start date or subaward date family member depending on requested label.
Award end date when applicable.
Issued date for loan rows.
Loan value for loan rows.
Subsidy cost for assistance rows.
CFDA/Assistance Listing number.
Assistance listing objects for assistance rows.
Primary assistance listing helper object.
Prime award display identifier returned for subaward rows.
Prime recipient name returned for subaward rows.
Prime award recipient id helper field.
Prime award internal id helper field for subaward rows. Prefer prime_award_generated_internal_id as the canonical_award_lookup_id when it is present.

Generated unique award id for the prime award behind a subaward row. When drilling from a subaward row into prime-award detail or funding workflows, treat this as the prime-award version of canonical_award_lookup_id.
Subaward amount for subaward-mode rows.
Subaward date.
Subaward identifier.
Subaward type label.
Subawardee name.
Disaster code list attached to returned award rows.
COVID-19 obligation measure for rows when requested.
COVID-19 outlay measure for rows when requested.
Infrastructure obligation measure for rows when requested.
Infrastructure outlay measure for rows when requested.
NAICS is returned as a structured object rather than a single scalar string.
PSC is returned as a structured object rather than a single scalar string.
Recipient Location is returned as a structured address/location object.
Primary Place of Performance is returned as a structured location object.
Sub-Recipient Location is returned as a structured location object.
Sub-Award Primary Place of Performance is returned as a structured location object.

pagination.strategy: page-number pagination with optional Elasticsearch search_after cursor pair

fields.description: Request page number for ordinary pagination.
Request page size.
Sequential cursor component echoed in page_metadata when another page is available.
Sequential cursor sort-value component echoed in page_metadata when another page is available.

pagination.notes: Observed page/limit pagination plus page_metadata cursor values. Source indicates both cursor fields must be replayed together for sequential search_after pagination.

notes: The current raw profile models filters only as a generic object, so nested advanced-search fields are not discoverable from the raw schema alone.
The current raw profile does not expose spending_level, subawards, or the search_after cursor pair even though docs and source support them.

quirks.statement: Result rows use API-defined field labels instead of a stable snake_case schema, and the exact label set depends on spending_level plus award type family.
quirks.impact: Callers must treat the fields array as part of the response contract, not just as a projection hint.

quirks.statement: Some requested labels map to structured response objects rather than scalars, including NAICS, PSC, Recipient Location, Primary Place of Performance, Sub-Recipient Location, and Sub-Award Primary Place of Performance.
quirks.impact: Do not assume every requested field value is a plain string or number.

quirks.statement: The same downstream award-handoff business key is labeled differently across related workflows: spending_by_award emits generated_internal_id, award detail guidance discusses generated_unique_award_id or generated_internal_id, and award funding accepts the same value under request field award_id.
quirks.impact: Normalize that cross-endpoint handoff value as canonical_award_lookup_id within this bundle, read it from the documented response-side semantic fact results[].canonical_award_lookup_id, and pass it unchanged into downstream award detail or award funding requests.

gaps.statement: The endpoint docs defer important nested object details such as location objects and time-period variants to shared search_filters documentation instead of fully defining them inline.
gaps.impact: Highly specific nested filters may need extra validation or shared-doc lookup before use.

risks.statement: Large result sets are expensive and the source enforces sequential search_after pagination for deep result windows.
risks.impact: Use bounded limit values and prefer downloads for bulk extraction workflows.

risks.statement: Requested field labels, sort compatibility, and valid award_type_codes interact with each other and can produce validation errors when mixed incorrectly.
risks.impact: Reuse known-good field sets and keep award families consistent within a request.

risks.statement: Live responses can include a warning that search time periods are currently limited to an earliest date of 2007-10-01.
risks.impact: Older historical analysis may need download endpoints instead of award search.

risks.statement: Display Award ID values are human-facing labels and are not the most reliable cross-endpoint handoff key when generated_internal_id is present on a prime-award row.
risks.impact: Using the display label instead of the documented response-side semantic fact canonical_award_lookup_id can make award-detail or funding drilldowns fragile.
